name: Halibut Piccata
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 6-8 inch frying pan over medium-high heat, stir minced garlic in 1/2 tablespoon olive oil until limp, about 2 minutes. Add wine, lemon juice, and capers. Simmer, uncovered, over high heat until reduced to 1/2 cup, 3-4 minutes. Keep sauce warm.
2. Rinse fish, pat dry, and cut into 6 equal portions. Rub fish with remaining olive oil, sprinkle with pepper, and arrange in a single layer in a 12x17 inch broiler pan (without rack).
3. Broil about 3 inches from the heat for 3 minutes. Turn fish over; sprinkle with cheese, and broil until opaque but still moist-looking in the center of thickest part (test by cutting), about 3 minutes longer. Transfer to a platter and pour sauce onto fish.
